components.value: V, S, M (beverage or food item, plus special reagents worth 25 gp)

area.value: 1 beverage or piece of food touched

description.value: You mix a malign spirit into a piece of food, a drink, or some other item to be consumed (including alchemical items, potions, elixirs, or similar items). The skin tag spell discharges when the item is consumed, affecting the creature that used the item unless it succeeds at a Fortitude save. The spirit embeds itself somewhere in the affected creatureâs skin, forming a painless set of wrinkles and tags of skin that looks vaguely like a face when the haunt activates. You are considered to have a body part from the affected creature for the purpose of scrying and similar divination spells. Once per day as a standard action, you can remotely cause the spirit to make the affected creatureâs skin crawl, causing the creature to become sickened for 1d4 rounds + 1 round per 2 caster levels you have (to a maximum of 5 extra rounds at 10th level) unless it succeeds at a Fortitude save. Alternatively, once per day you can remotely use a hex, a mind-affecting spell, or a necromancy spell that causes disease or possession on the affected creature. Each time you use the remote sickening or spell or hex effect, the spellâs haunt -like manifestation occurs during that round. HAUNT STATISTICS Notice Perception DC 20 (to taste a hint of rot) or DC 25 (if the affected creature is wearing armor or heavy clothing); hp 5 hp; Trigger conditional (using the item or the spellâs remote effect); Reset none Get this book AND Support this site!
